title: [CI:DOCS] Fix spelling rootfull -> rootful
body:
If I understand correctly the spelling "rootful" should be prefered over "rootfull" (see
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8787#issuecomment-749026276).

This PR fixes
./docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md

There is one file that still has the wrong spelling 
./vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/config/containers.conf

```
[esjolund@laptop podman]$ grep rootfull ./vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/config/containers.conf
#       rootfull "unix://run/podman/podman.sock (Default)
#       remote rootfull ssh://root@10.10.1.136:22/run/podman/podman.sock
[esjolund@laptop podman]$ 
```
But I leave those out because they are in vendored code.

The recommendation "_Do not change vendored code._" can be seen here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8787#discussion_r546678504

Signed-off-by: Erik Sjölund <erik.sjolund@gmail.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *eriksjolund* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **jwhonce** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @jwhonce` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
The decision was the other way around rootfull is correct just like rootless.
@rhatdan I see, both spellings can be used. No need for this PR then. I'll close it.
